mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate signals easing discount rates and improving liquidity conditions, which lifts equity valuation multiples and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in easing episodes or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 while FEDFUNDS is falling.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates and policy restraint tighten financial conditions, compress risk appetite, and predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above 3.5% while FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time or if inflation-plus-tight-policy episodes fail to outperform a zero-return benchmark in sign accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor income growth and higher recession risk, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not lower when UNRATE is trending up versus flat/down periods, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp PAYEMS contraction accompanied by a rising UNRATE identifies payroll deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off repricing, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls by more than 150 thousand over 2 months and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-loss plus unemployment-rise trigger does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of occurrences or if either leg alone fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS reflects stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should raise near-term earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth does not coincide with above-median 20-day SPX returns or if the effect disappears in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST contracts after a prior decline in GDPC1, the combination signals housing-led demand weakening and rising recession sensitivity, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below its 6-month average and GDPC1 has slowed versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-and-GDP slowdown condition does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the GDP leg adds no incremental predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: An uptrend in INDPRO signals stronger industrial production and better cyclical earnings prospects, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets reprice growth expectations quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive following INDPRO rises, or if the signal fails to beat random sign accuracy over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL remains subdued, the economy is growing without renewed inflation pressure, allowing multiples to expand and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases over 3 months and CPIAUCSL inflation stays below 3.0% year-over-year
failure_condition: Reject if the joint growth-with-low-inflation condition does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases or if the effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL is excluded.
